name: Coins & Currency of Orlando
description_short: Family-owned coins, precious metals, and jewelry buyer/seller in Orlando with 20+ years of experience. Offers competitive pricing for collectors and bullion investors.
description_long:
Coins & Currency of Orlando is a family-owned and operated business located at 2603 Curry Ford Road in Orlando, Florida, that has been serving the central Florida corridor for over 20 years. The company specializes in numismatic coins, precious metals (gold, silver, platinum, palladium, and rhodium), jewelry, and diamonds of all sizes. They position themselves as community-focused business leaders with transparent, nationally competitive pricing.

The company offers both buying and selling services for coins and precious metals. On the buying side, they cater to both collectors seeking numismatic or collector-value coins and bullion investors focused on intrinsic metal content. On the selling side, they provide professional appraisals and evaluations, emphasizing market transparency and current pricing. Their website indicates they offer competitive spreads on bullion and handle rare coins, jewelry appraisals, and diamond evaluations. They also note they have an e-commerce platform under development.

What distinguishes Coins & Currency of Orlando is their stated emphasis on relationship-building and knowledge-sharing with customers. Multiple reviews highlight transparency in their evaluation process, with staff explaining reference materials and pricing methodologies. Owners Priscilla and Josh are mentioned by name across reviews. The business emphasizes discretion, professionalism, and treating customers with respect regardless of transaction size. They advertise themselves as industry leaders offering the "tightest bullion spreads in Orlando."

However, as a pawn-shop/precious metals dealer, this business is primarily transactional rather than providing ongoing financial services. Customer evaluations are entirely positive (all 5-star reviews shown), but this may reflect selection bias on their website. The business model depends on commodity market fluctuations and customer perception of fair value. No information is provided about authentication processes, grading standards used, or dispute resolution procedures. Buyers and sellers should understand they are receiving subjective appraisals subject to market conditions.
pros: 20+ years operating in central Florida with established reputation for trust and integrity
Dual-focus business serves both rare coin collectors and bullion investors with competitive pricing
Staff provides transparent explanations of pricing methodologies and reference materials used
Family ownership and local operation; owners Priscilla and Josh named in customer reviews
Handles full range of precious metals including gold, silver, platinum, palladium, and rhodium
Accommodates out-of-state customers and resolves customer issues proactively (per reviews)
E-commerce platform in development to expand beyond in-person transactions
cons: No information about coin grading standards, authentication processes, or certifications used
Appraisals are subjective and subject to market fluctuations; no published pricing formulas
Limited detail on dispute resolution or return policies for transactions
No information about insurance, storage, or security for high-value transactions
Business model is transactional rather than ongoing financial services; no recurring value
best_for: Coin and numismatic collectors in central Florida looking to buy or sell rare coins
Bullion investors seeking to trade precious metals with competitive spreads
Individuals with inherited jewelry or coin collections needing professional appraisal and fair pricing
Out-of-state customers seeking a trustworthy dealer for mail-in precious metals transactions
diagnosis: Coins & Currency of Orlando is best for collectors and bullion investors in central Florida seeking to buy or sell precious metals and rare coins with a trustworthy, transparent dealer. The main caveat is that this is a commodity transaction business, not a financial services provider—prices fluctuate with markets, appraisals are subjective, and there is no ongoing customer relationship or protection beyond the individual transaction.
services: Rare coin buying and selling for collectors
Bullion coin buying and selling (gold, silver, platinum, palladium, rhodium)
Professional market evaluations and transparency reporting
